free_text: Measurement and analysis of nuclear gamma-ray
production cross sections in proton interactions with
Mg, Si, and Fe nuclei abundant in astrophysical sites
over the incident energy range E=30-66 MeV
Targets mounted onto rectangular Al frames and placed
on a ladder allowing four target positions
(beam viewer, empty frame, two frames with targets)
clover detectors (four at 90 deg and four at 135 deg)
placaed 17.6 cm from the target to measure gamma-rays
at 85, 95, 130 and 140 deg which subtended ~11% of
Energy calibration was done with 137Cs, 60Co and 152Eu
standard sources up to 1408 keV, and also with
The relative energy resolution was less than 0.23%
Detection efficiency calibration was done with the
above mentioned standard sources and extrapolation
to higher energies by GEANT4 simulation.
Isolated Faraday cup located 3 m downstream
of the reaction chamber and placed 1.5 m deep inside
- beam related background by using the empty target
- room background runs without beam and without a
- attenuation due to the geometry of the detection
426 keV: less than 3% at 66 MeV and 1% at 30 MeV
426keV: less than 9% (12% at 42 MeV / 5% at 30 MeV)
585 keV: less than 1% (2% at 42 and 66 MeV)
